name: youtube-automation
description: Automate YouTube tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): upload videos, manage playlists, search content, get analytics, and handle comments. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Upload videos to YouTube with custom metadata including titles, descriptions, tags, and privacy settings
Search YouTube content for videos, channels, and playlists using advanced query syntax
Create and manage playlists including adding videos and listing contents
Retrieve channel and video statistics including view counts, subscriber counts, and engagement metrics
Manage channel subscriptions and retrieve video comments for analysis
Update video metadata and thumbnails after initial upload
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 70 static findings are false positives from Markdown code formatting. The file contains only documentation describing YouTube API workflows via Rube MCP, not executable code. No security risks detected.
valueStatement: Save hours of manual YouTube management work by automating video uploads, playlist organization, and analytics tracking through Claude. This skill uses Composio's YouTube toolkit to handle API interactions directly in your workflow.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active YouTube OAuth authentication before use
YouTube API has daily quota limits (default 10,000 units) that can throttle high-volume operations
Video uploads require files to be hosted via S3 with proper s3key formatting, not local file paths
Cannot bypass YouTube's content policies, upload limits, or API restrictions regardless of skill capabilities

useCases.title: Content Creator Upload Workflow
useCases.description: Upload videos to YouTube with optimized titles, descriptions, and tags. Set appropriate privacy status and add custom thumbnails without manual API calls.

useCases.title: Channel Analytics Dashboard
useCases.description: Track channel growth and video performance by retrieving statistics, view counts, and engagement metrics. Analyze multiple videos in batch for comparative reporting.

useCases.title: Playlist Management System
useCases.description: Organize video libraries by creating themed playlists, bulk-adding videos, and maintaining structured collections for audience navigation.

promptTemplates.title: Upload Video with Metadata
promptTemplates.prompt: Upload my video file from S3 (s3key: 'my-video.mp4') to YouTube with title 'My Tutorial', description 'Learn how to...', tags ['tutorial', 'how-to'], category 22, and set as unlisted.
promptTemplates.scenario: Upload a new video with specific metadata and privacy settings

promptTemplates.title: Search and Analyze Content
promptTemplates.prompt: Search YouTube for 'machine learning tutorial' videos, get the top 10 results, then retrieve detailed statistics for each video including view counts and engagement metrics.
promptTemplates.scenario: Find and analyze competing content in a specific niche

promptTemplates.title: Create Curated Playlist
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new playlist called 'Python Tutorials 2024', then search for 'python tutorial' and add the top 20 results to this playlist.
promptTemplates.scenario: Build organized playlists from search results

promptTemplates.title: Channel Performance Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Get statistics for my channel (@MyChannel), list all videos from the uploads playlist, retrieve detailed metrics for each video, and compile a performance report.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate comprehensive analytics reports for channel management

bestPractices: Always search tools first with RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before using any YouTube tools to get current schemas and parameter requirements
Prefer UPDATE_VIDEO over UPLOAD_VIDEO for metadata-only changes to conserve API quota (upload costs 1600 units vs update costs much less)
Use batch operations (GET_VIDEO_DETAILS_BATCH) for retrieving multiple video statistics instead of individual calls to reduce quota usage
Handle pagination by checking nextPageToken in responses and making follow-up requests until the token is absent
antiPatterns: Passing channel IDs (UC...) as playlist IDs - convert uploads by replacing 'UC' prefix with 'UU' to get the correct playlist ID
Exceeding the 5000 byte limit on video descriptions by counting characters instead - multibyte characters consume more than one byte
Using items[].id from playlist listings as videoId - use items[].snippet.resourceId.videoId instead
Calling upload tools when only metadata changes are needed - use UPDATE_VIDEO to avoid wasting quota

faq.question: What are the prerequisites for using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP connected with RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS available, and an active YouTube connection via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with the 'youtube' toolkit. Complete Google OAuth authentication before running workflows.

faq.question: How do I upload a video file?
faq.answer: Videos must be hosted on S3 and passed as an object with name, mimetype, and s3key properties. Local file paths and direct URLs are not supported by the YouTube API tool.

faq.question: What are the YouTube API quota limits?
faq.answer: Default daily quota is 10,000 units. Upload costs 1600 units, search costs 100 units, and list operations cost 1 unit. Plan workflows accordingly to avoid hitting limits.

faq.question: How do I get all videos from a channel?
faq.answer: Get the channel ID, convert 'UC' prefix to 'UU' to get the uploads playlist ID, then use LIST_PLAYLIST_ITEMS with pagination to retrieve all videos.

faq.question: Can I update video metadata after uploading?
faq.answer: Yes, use UPDATE_VIDEO to modify title, description, tags, and privacy status without re-uploading. This saves quota compared to uploading a new version.

faq.question: Why do my batch video details return fewer items than requested?
faq.answer: Private, deleted, or region-restricted videos may not return details. Always parse responses defensively as the API may omit unavailable videos from batch results.
